english: walks in the same ways (as his predecessors), a very frq. epithet of the Buddhas. — γśégs-bskyés (gshegs bskyes) parting-(beer-) cup, parting-feast or treat Mil. — γśégs-zón (gshegs zon) Cs. a banquet or dinner after the death of a great person.

rarely γśed (gshed), 1. executioner, hangman Stg.; γśed-ma skó-ba (gshed ma sko ba) to engage a hangman, i.e. to pay a murderer Glr.; fig.: p'an tsun γcíg-gi γśed γcíg-gis byá-ste (phan tshun gcig gi gshed gcig gis bya ste) Wdṅ. prob. means: one destroying the other. — 2. in a special sense: gods of vengeance, tormenting the condemned in hell, or fighting against evil spirits, drag-γśèd (drag gshed) Schl.

Glr., the founder of the Bon-religion, his full name being bon γśen-rabs-γyuṅ-druṅ (bon gshen rabs g.yung drung) C. prob. identic with the Chinese philosopher Lao-tse — ye-γśen (ye gshen) Mil. id. — sgrub-γśén (sgrub gshen) the Bon-doctrine (opp. to dam-čós (dam chos) Mil.)

B., C.: wet, wetness; γśér-ba (gsher ba), γśer-pa (gsher pa), γśér-po (gsher po) adj., γśér-bar gyúr-ba (gsher bar gyur ba) to get thoroughly wet, to be drenched Dzl., to get moist, to be moistened; γśér-bar byéd-pa (gsher bar byed pa) C., B., *śér-pu čó-če* (sher pu co ce) W. to wet, to moisten. In C. and in B. γśer-ba (gsher ba) seems to be mainly used for wet, in W. for moist.

sbst. and adj. v. the preceding article. II. vb. 1. to ask for, beg for, śèr-te t'ob soṅ (sher te thob song) I got it by asking for it (I did not buy it) W. — 2. to ask *śér-len-pa* (sher len pa) (lit. γśér-len-pa (gsher len pa)) to interrogate, to question, to try (judicially); as partic. and sbst.; the examining or criminal judge C. — 3. = γśór-ba (gshor ba) to measure C.
pf. γśos (gshos), 1. to pour out, to pour away C., prob. the same as bśo-ba (bsho ba). — 2 (?) *k'a śós-te k'yer* (kha shos te khyer) he has alienated him, enticed him to join his own party Ld.
lever *śó-mo gyáb-pa* (sho mo rgyab pa) C., W., *taṅ-če* (tang ce) W., *śó-mo kán-pa* (sho mo kan pa) W., *kémpa* (kem pa) C. to put a lever to (a thing).

sbst. v. śóg-pa (shog pa). II. vb., also bśóg-pa (bshog pa), γśág-pa (gshag pa), γśég-pa (gsheg pa), γśégs-pa (gshegs pa), pf. γśags (gshags), bśags (bshags), fut. γśag (gshag), bśag (bshag), imp. γśog (gshog), Pth., 1. to cleave, to split, śiṅ (shing) wood, rnám-par (rnam par) entirely, tsál-pa bźir (tshal pa bzhir) into four pieces Glr., dúm-bur (dum bur) into pieces; sgo γśág-pa (sgo gshag pa) to break open a door (with a hatchet) Pth.; to break or pierce through, dkyil (dkyil) through the middle Mil., gaṅs bśóg-pa (gangs bshog pa) Mil. to break through the snow, by means of yaks sent in advance to beat a path (v. Huc.), lam bśog-pa (lam bshog pa) in a gen. sense: to beat a path; lam mi śog (lam mi shog) or ma śog-par dug (ma shog par dug) Glr. the road is not practicable; sóg-les γśég-pa (sog les gsheg pa) Thg. to saw lengthwise; — γśeg-pa (gsheg pa) is also used for: to rend, to tear, to make a rent or slit into a dress etc. C., W. — 2. to confess, sdig-pa (sdig pa), nyés-pa (nyes pa), ltuṅ-ba (ltung ba) to confess a sin, and thus to expiate it, which two, according to the views of a Buddhist, are always united, at least as it regards lighter transgressions. Hence sdig-pa bśags (sdig pa bshags) frq. means: the sin is atoned for, is blotted out, and γśeg-pa (gsheg pa) is the usual word for 'to forgive'. sdig-bśags (sdig bshags) atonement, expiation, sbrul bśád-pai-sdig-bśags-su (sbrul bshad pai sdig bshags su) as an atonement for having killed a serpent Glr.; mtol-bśags (mtol bshags) = sdig-bśags (sdig bshags), mtol-bśags-la śa-kóg búl-ba (mtol bshags la sha kog bul ba) to offer a killed animal (a sheep) as an atonement Mil.; sdig-bśags-smón-lam (sdig bshags smon lam) Glr. penitential prayer.
Cs.: = skyón-pa (skyon pa) to put on (?).
Sch. a basin or reservoir of water, seems to be not much known; but in Zam. yúr-bai γśor (yur bai gshor) is to be found.

vb., C. also γśér-ba (gsher ba), pf. fut. bśar (bshar), bśor (bshor), 1. to count, e.g. sheep, by letting them pass one by one through one's hands, the beads of a rosary (through one's fingers), hence čos-brjod-bśar-sbyaṅ maṅ-po byed (chos brjod bshar sbyang mang po byed) to read prayers etc. (cf. sbyaṅ-ba (sbyang ba), 3). — 2. to measure, bres (bres) by the peck Lex.; to weigh; γśér-la rá-gan γśár-ba (gsher la ra gan gshar ba) to weigh out (to exchange) brass
